architecture.summary: The system steers LLM text generation towards specific styles (sentiment, emotion, writing style) by computing style vectors from recorded activations of input texts in a target style, then adding these vectors to hidden layer activations during decoding. Style vectors are extracted from layer activations without training; during generation, they are directly added to the model's own hidden states at specified layers to modulate output style.

biblio.title: Style Vectors for Steering Generative Large Language Models

claims.statement: style vectors can be simply computed from recorded layer activations for input texts in a specific style in contrast to more complex training-based approaches
activation engineering using style vectors can influence the style of generated text in a nuanced and parameterisable way
style vectors are distinguished from prompt engineering as a mechanism for steering LLM output
